jokes.text: The fundamentals of business. Mental is a part of the word. I have underlined it.
jokes.setup: Michael is giving a business presentation about fundamentals
jokes.punchline: He literally underlines 'mental' in 'fundamentals' as if it's meaningful
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael Scott pseudo-intellectual wordplay that reveals his lack of business acumen while maintaining his confidence

jokes.text: Because you're mental if you don't have a good time. You have to enjoy it. The fun is in it.
jokes.setup: Continuing the 'mental' explanation
jokes.punchline: He doubles down on the nonsensical wordplay connection
jokes.explanation: Extension of the mental/fundamental joke but slightly weaker on its own

jokes.text: Small things... Peas, ball bearings, dimes.
jokes.setup: Asked what topics can be used for small talk
jokes.punchline: Instead of conversation topics, he lists literally small objects
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with economy of language - audience expects conversation topics but gets physical objects

jokes.text: I caught my son taking a dump on the upper part of the toilet. He calls it an upper-decker.
jokes.setup: Michael asks Meredith about her weekend for a business demonstration
jokes.punchline: She shares inappropriate bathroom story in professional setting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith character moment - inappropriate overshare that derails Michael's professional demonstration

jokes.text: What you people don't know about business I could fill a book with. Then do it. Write a book.
jokes.setup: Michael brags about his business knowledge using a common expression
jokes.punchline: Jim takes it literally and suggests he actually write the book
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that exposes the emptiness of Michael's boast

jokes.text: Over 1 billion sold. More than the Bible. I'm not surprised.
jokes.setup: Michael imagining his hypothetical business book's success
jokes.punchline: He claims it would outsell the Bible and acts unsurprised by this
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusional self-importance perfectly captured in his casual confidence about outselling the Bible

jokes.text: Your schedule just says nine till noon is creative space. Do you know how creative space works?
jokes.setup: Erin questions Michael's schedule being interrupted
jokes.punchline: Michael acts like 'creative space' is a sophisticated business concept when it's just empty time
jokes.explanation: Michael's pretentious reaction to being called out on his empty schedule

jokes.text: You don't have anything in the afternoon. It just says, 'free play.'
jokes.setup: Michael claims to cancel his afternoon
jokes.punchline: Erin reveals his schedule literally says 'free play' like a kindergartener's
jokes.explanation: 'Free play' perfectly captures Michael's childish approach to management while Erin delivers it matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Sorry, crazy day. You're seeing how the sausage gets made. Come in the conference room, and I will show you a finished sausage.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to impress the insurance salesman
jokes.punchline: He misuses the 'sausage making' metaphor and promises to show a 'finished sausage'
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt to sound sophisticated backfires with awkward metaphor extension

jokes.text: Jim's gone on his honeymoon. So I started borrowing his office to fart in. Then one day, I came in, and I just stayed.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why he's in Jim's office
jokes.punchline: He admits he originally used it just for farting but then moved in
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic progression from inappropriate office use to full occupation

jokes.text: My home sucks.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why he likes Jim's office better than his home
jokes.punchline: Simple, matter-of-fact admission about his living situation
jokes.explanation: Dwight's blunt honesty delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: Think you're right. Definitely looks suspicious. And his southern italian heritage raises some flags.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether the insurance salesman seems suspicious
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately jumps to ethnic profiling
jokes.explanation: Dwight's casual prejudice fits his character but relies on stereotypes

jokes.text: There is no such thing as monsters. Mobsters are.
jokes.setup: Dwight says mobsters are threats, Michael mishears as monsters
jokes.punchline: Michael corrects about monsters while Dwight clarifies mobsters
jokes.explanation: Simple misunderstanding creates brief confusion

jokes.text: More trunk space. Or should I say corpse space?
jokes.setup: Discussing why the insurance salesman drives an SUV
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately connects it to corpse transportation
jokes.explanation: Dwight's morbid interpretation fits his character's conspiracy mindset

jokes.text: Guys, I drive an SUV. Does that mean I'm in the mob? No, not that by itself.
jokes.setup: Michael tries to disprove the SUV theory using himself as example
jokes.punchline: Dwight's response suggests other evidence makes Michael suspicious
jokes.explanation: Dwight's implication that Michael could be mobster based on other factors

jokes.text: And he also vaguely threatened me with testicular cancer.
jokes.setup: Michael listing the insurance salesman's threats
jokes.punchline: He misinterprets insurance coverage mention as a threat
jokes.explanation: Michael's paranoid misinterpretation of insurance sales pitch as personal threat

jokes.text: Pam and Jim are on their honeymoon, so there's not the usual balance between sane and others. The coalition for reason is extremely weak.
jokes.setup: Oscar explaining the office dynamic in talking head
jokes.punchline: He describes the office as needing a 'coalition for reason' against the others
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the show's character dynamics with military-style language

jokes.text: First of all, there's no such thing as 'the mafia.' What you have are specific families.
jokes.setup: Ryan being asked about mafia knowledge from living in New York
jokes.punchline: He immediately acts like an expert with pretentious corrections
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan character moment - pretentious expertise about organized crime

jokes.text: John Gotti, you idiot. It's a completely different name. What mobster would change his name from Gotti to Grotti?
jokes.setup: Kevin suggests Grotti sounds like Gotti
jokes.punchline: Ryan's condescending logic about why a mobster wouldn't make that name change
jokes.explanation: Ryan's arrogant dismissal with sound logic about criminal name changes

jokes.text: 'R' is among the most menacing of sounds. That's why they call it murder, not 'mukduk.'
jokes.setup: Dwight defending that Grotti is more menacing than Gotti
jokes.punchline: His made-up linguistic theory with invented word 'mukduk'
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Dwight logic with perfect invented word 'mukduk' - shows his weird intelligence and complete confidence in absurd theories

jokes.text: Too many different words coming at me from too many different sentences.
jokes.setup: Multiple people giving Michael contradictory advice about the mafia situation
jokes.punchline: Michael's overwhelmed, childish way of expressing confusion
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to process multiple inputs expressed like a child

jokes.text: Unless someone very close to us is in immediate physical danger, you should not be calling us.
jokes.setup: Oscar calls Jim and Pam on their honeymoon about Michael's mafia fears
jokes.punchline: Jim sets very specific boundaries for honeymoon interruptions
jokes.explanation: Jim's reasonable but firm boundary-setting shows his maturity vs office chaos

jokes.text: That is the stupidest thing you could do right now.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests calling the police about the mafia threat
jokes.punchline: Dwight's immediate, definitive rejection shows his mob movie knowledge
jokes.explanation: Dwight's expertise from mob movies treating situation as real

jokes.text: Not only that, but if they find out you snitched, you get a dead horse's chopped-off head in your bed.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why not to call police
jokes.punchline: He misremembers the famous Godfather scene with added gruesome details
jokes.explanation: Dwight's garbled pop culture reference with unnecessary gore addition

jokes.text: Criminals are like raccoons. You give them a taste of cat food, pretty soon, they'll be back for the whole cat.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why giving in to criminals is bad
jokes.punchline: His animal analogy escalates from cat food to eating the whole cat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight analogy that starts logical but veers into absurdist territory with perfect confidence

jokes.text: Trust me, I have bullied a lot of people.
jokes.setup: Dwight giving advice about standing up to bullies
jokes.punchline: He casually admits to being a bully himself as credentials
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - complete lack of self-awareness about his own behavior while giving advice

jokes.text: If I'm gonna back you up, I need a weapon without drawing suspicion. So I'm a mechanic with a tire thing.
jokes.setup: Dwight preparing for the meeting with the insurance salesman
jokes.punchline: His elaborate disguise plan involving tire iron and mechanic identity
jokes.explanation: Dwight's overly complex plan for simple backup role shows his tactical overthinking

jokes.text: Do you know how to use it? To change tires? No. But... it's metal. I could hit somebody.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if Dwight knows how to use the tire iron
jokes.punchline: Dwight admits no mechanical knowledge but sees violent potential
jokes.explanation: Dwight's honest admission about his real intentions with the tool

jokes.text: The bathroom checks out clean. Nothing behind the toilet except this roach motel.
jokes.setup: Dwight scouting the restaurant for security
jokes.punchline: His military-style reconnaissance of a normal restaurant bathroom
jokes.explanation: Dwight treating casual lunch meeting like military operation

jokes.text: You want to separate the head from the thorax.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions killing a roach
jokes.punchline: Dwight gives overly technical advice about insect anatomy
jokes.explanation: Dwight's unnecessary expertise applied to simple pest control

jokes.text: Hello, Mr. Halpert? I'm calling from The identity theft department at Capital One.
jokes.setup: Dwight calling Jim while he's on his honeymoon
jokes.punchline: He's pretending to be from credit card fraud department
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight prank continuing their rivalry even during Jim's honeymoon

jokes.text: I'll have linguini, red sauce on the side. If the sauce does not come on the side, I will send it back.
jokes.setup: Michael ordering at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: His unnecessarily specific demands with threats to send food back
jokes.explanation: Michael trying to appear tough through aggressive restaurant ordering

jokes.text: I will have the gabba-gool. The what? The gabba-gool.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to order Italian food to fit in
jokes.punchline: His mispronunciation of capicola as 'gabba-gool' confuses waitress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael Scott cultural appropriation attempt with confident mispronunciation

jokes.text: Bring him the gabba-gool!
jokes.setup: Waitress doesn't understand Michael's order
jokes.punchline: Dwight aggressively demands the nonexistent dish
jokes.explanation: Dwight's aggressive support of Michael's embarrassing mistake

jokes.text: If the salad is on top, I send it back.
jokes.setup: Michael continuing his specific restaurant demands
jokes.punchline: Another arbitrary rule about food presentation
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Michael's trying-to-be-tough restaurant behavior

jokes.text: I wouldn't last in jail, Oscar. I'm not like you. You would love jail.
jokes.setup: Dwight worried about jail time for identity fraud
jokes.punchline: His awkward implication about Oscar's sexuality and prison
jokes.explanation: Dwight's awkward stereotyping creates uncomfortable moment

jokes.text: My battery's dead. I've got my kid. Can you please help? Yes, I can.
jokes.setup: Woman asks Dwight (in mechanic costume) for car help
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately agrees despite having no mechanical knowledge
jokes.explanation: Dwight's commitment to his fake identity despite complete incompetence

jokes.text: You got a leaky spark tube. So your car's totaled. You're just gonna want to get a refund on that.
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to diagnose car problems
jokes.punchline: His completely made-up diagnosis and ridiculous conclusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake mechanical terminology ('leaky spark tube') with absurd conclusion about totaled car

jokes.text: I work exclusively on motorcycles.
jokes.setup: Dwight failing to help with car
jokes.punchline: He creates another layer of his fake mechanic identity
jokes.explanation: Dwight's desperate attempt to maintain credibility by narrowing his fake expertise

jokes.text: This Italian car. And I was driving it, and it kept telling me how much it needed oil. But I wouldn't give it any oil. And then, one day, it exploded. And it killed everyone.
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to tell a threatening story to the insurance salesman
jokes.punchline: His bizarre metaphor about a car that talks and mass death
jokes.explanation: Completely unhinged Dwight intimidation attempt with talking car and mass casualties

jokes.text: Aren't you a mechanic? Why wouldn't you put oil in the car? It was before my technical training.
jokes.setup: Insurance salesman questions Dwight's mechanic story logic
jokes.punchline: Dwight has to invent timeline for his fake expertise
jokes.explanation: Dwight's quick thinking to maintain his cover story despite obvious plot hole

jokes.text: It wasn't a snap decision. You were sitting there for an hour. Do you know what snap decision means? It means like this.
jokes.setup: Michael claims he made a snap decision
jokes.punchline: Dwight corrects him with literal demonstration of 'snap'
jokes.explanation: Dwight's literal interpretation with finger snap demonstration

jokes.text: I had to tell the hotel that it was a medical emergency. I chose massive coronary, because you told me that your dad had a bad heart.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining how he got Jim's hotel number
jokes.punchline: His elaborate lie using Jim's family medical history
jokes.explanation: Kevin's disturbing use of personal information for deception shows his questionable judgment

jokes.text: Bermuda Triangle.
jokes.setup: Jim can't hear Kevin due to bad phone connection
jokes.punchline: He blames supernatural phenomenon rather than technology
jokes.explanation: Jim's dry humor about phone problems with perfect economy

jokes.text: What about this cash for clunkers thing?
jokes.setup: Michael asking Oscar about getting out of insurance payments
jokes.punchline: He randomly mentions unrelated government program
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperate grasping at any government program for financial help

jokes.text: We have let Michael down. And it's 85% your fault.
jokes.setup: Dwight and Andy discussing Michael's situation
jokes.punchline: Dwight's precise percentage blame assignment
jokes.explanation: Dwight's mathematical precision applied to emotional situations

jokes.text: Look at his life. Broke. Living in fear. No friends, dead end job. Some of that existed before.
jokes.setup: Dwight listing what's wrong with Michael's life
jokes.punchline: Andy's honest admission that most problems pre-existed
jokes.explanation: Brutal honest assessment of Michael's life with perfect timing

jokes.text: Are you saying that we surgically remove the fear center from Michael's brain?
jokes.setup: Dwight suggesting making Michael fearless
jokes.punchline: Andy's literal interpretation involves brain surgery
jokes.explanation: Andy's immediate jump to extreme medical procedure as solution

jokes.text: If there is one thing I hate more than the mafia, it is a liar. I wish the mafia would go out and kill all the liars. And bury them in my yard.
jokes.setup: Michael learning the insurance salesman was just pushy, not mafia
jokes.punchline: His convoluted logic about preferring mafia to liars
jokes.explanation: Michael's twisted moral hierarchy with casual murder fantasy

jokes.text: You suck! And I won't buy your stupid insurance.
jokes.setup: Michael continues his confrontation
jokes.punchline: His insults remain at elementary school level
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Michael's childish insults. Shows his limitations in confrontation but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You suck! And I won't buy your stupid insurance.
jokes.setup: Michael confronting the insurance salesman on phone
jokes.punchline: His childish insults when he thinks he has upper hand
jokes.explanation: Michael's immature response when feeling powerful, fairly predictable

jokes.text: We just told you he wasn't mafia so you wouldn't be scared. You successfully backed down the mob.
jokes.setup: Michael feeling proud about standing up to salesman
jokes.punchline: They reveal they lied to give him confidence, then lie again about his success
jokes.explanation: Perfect Office moment - they immediately create new lie to maintain Michael's confidence

jokes.text: Just to be clear, he backed down an insurance agent from Mutual of Harrisburg.
jokes.setup: Michael bragging about his mafia confrontation
jokes.punchline: Oscar deflates the story with mundane truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar moment - deflating Michael's fantasy with precise, boring reality

jokes.text: Coffee. Not from the kitchen. Stop and shop. If it's not stop and shop, I send it back.
jokes.setup: Michael ordering coffee with new confidence
jokes.punchline: He applies his restaurant demanding behavior to coffee orders
jokes.explanation: Callback to Michael's demanding restaurant behavior but with diminishing returns

jokes.text: Coffee. Not from the kitchen. Stop and shop. If it's not stop and shop, I send it back. Large. If it's a medium, I send it back. If it's an extra-large, I send it back.
jokes.setup: Michael orders coffee back at the office
jokes.punchline: Returns to his impossibly demanding ordering style from the restaurant
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier demanding orders but less effective without the intimidation context. Shows Michael's continued control issues.

jokes.text: Our credit card has been canceled, and we have to deal with that, and I really can't handle the fact that you're calling us here!
jokes.setup: Kevin calls Jim and Pam again on honeymoon
jokes.punchline: Pam's frustrated explanation of their actual problems vs office nonsense
jokes.explanation: Pam's justified frustration at office chaos interrupting their honeymoon

jokes.text: I will make that my top priority.
jokes.setup: Kevin asks Pam to tell Jim 'hi'
jokes.punchline: Pam's sarcastic response while clearly annoyed
jokes.explanation: Pam's perfectly delivered sarcasm showing her irritation
